steps.prompt: Which word is the main verb?

hints.populi: 'populi' is a noun in the genitive meaning 'of the people'

hints.to eat: 'to eat' is 'edere' — 'Est' here is from 'esse' (to be)
hints.to go: 'to go' is 'ire' — 'Est' is the 3rd person singular of 'esse'
hints.to stand: 'to stand' is 'stare' — do not confuse with 'esse'

hints.Future indicative: Future of 'esse' would be 'erit' — 'est' is present
hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive of 'esse' would be 'sit' — 'est' is indicative
hints.Perfect indicative: Perfect of 'esse' would be 'fuit' — 'est' is present

hints.edo, edere, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat) — 'Est' here is from 'esse'
hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the parts of 'stare' (to stand) — a different verb
hints.eo, ire, ivi, itum: These are the parts of 'ire' (to go) — not 'esse'

steps.prompt: What case is the first 'res' (res publica)?

hints.Accusative: Accusative would be 'rem publicam' — 'res publica' is nominative as the subject
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 're publica' — this is the nominative subject
hints.Genitive: Genitive would be 'rei publicae' — 'res publica' here is nominative

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns typically end in -a — 'res' is a classic 5th declension noun
hints.3rd: 3rd declension nouns have varied endings — 'res, rei' follows the 5th declension pattern
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns end in -us/-u — 'res' belongs to the 5th declension

steps.prompt: What case is the second 'res' (res populi)?

hints.Accusative: Accusative would be 'rem' — this 'res' is the predicate nominative after 'est'
hints.Genitive: Genitive would be 'rei' — 'res' here is nominative as the predicate
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 're' — this is the predicate nominative

hints.Nominative: Nominative singular is 'populus' — 'populi' is genitive singular
hints.Dative: Dative singular would be 'populo' — 'populi' indicates possession (genitive)
hints.Vocative: Vocative singular would be 'popule' — 'populi' is genitive

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'populus, populi' is 2nd declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension genitive would end in -is — 'populi' is the 2nd declension genitive
hints.4th: 4th declension genitive would end in -us — 'populi' is 2nd declension

steps.prompt: What does Cicero define with this sentence?
steps.target: The republic as the property of the people
choices: The republic as the property of the people
The people as servants of the state
The republic as a form of monarchy

hints.The people as servants of the state: Cicero says the opposite — the republic belongs TO the people, not the other way around
hints.The republic as a form of monarchy: Cicero is defining res publica broadly as communal property, not as monarchy
hints.Public affairs as private matters: 'Publica' means public — Cicero defines the state as belonging to the people collectively

translation: The republic is the property of the people.
